steps.title: Use slope for additional cost
steps.content: The cost for each additional mile is the slope of the line: how much the total cost increases when $x$ increases by $1$ mile. Do not use the total cost of one ride as the per-mile cost.

steps.title: Read two clear points
steps.content: The graph shows the line passing through $(1,5)$ and $(2,7)$. Entering those two points in Desmos and running a linear regression gives the slope directly.

steps.title: Compute the slope
steps.content: $$m=\frac{7-5}{2-1}=2$$ So the cab ride cost increases by $2.00$ for each additional mile.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The additional-mile cost is $2.00$, which matches choice A. Choice C is the flat fee, and choice D is the total cost for a $1$-mile ride, not the per-mile increase.
